developer.comment: Founded 2022 by Igor Ostrovsky and Guy Gur-Ari; raised $227M Series B announced April 2024.

first-release.comment: Emerged from stealth on 2024-04-24 with its public launch. Agent mode reached GA for VS Code later (late 2025).

status.comment: Actively developed; VS Code extension at ~759k installs as of 2026-06-02.

license.comment: Closed-source commercial product. The public auggie CLI repo is only a shell installer/wrapper; the agent core is proprietary.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent positioned for large/complex enterprise codebases via its Context Engine, but not niche-specialized.

interface.comment: VS Code & JetBrains extensions, Auggie CLI, a web app (app.augmentcode.com), and the Intent Mac desktop workspace.

host-ides.comment: Ships as extensions for VS Code and all JetBrains IDEs, a Vim/Neovim integration, and the Auggie terminal CLI.

open-source.comment: The agent itself is closed-source; the public auggie GitHub repo is a shell installer/wrapper, not the core implementation.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Primarily IDE extensions plus the Auggie CLI; Remote Agents run as a cloud service.

default-model-provider.comment: Model picker offers Claude, GPT, and Gemini families plus the Prism router; no single default provider.

model-choice.comment: Model dropdown in VS Code/JetBrains and /model in Auggie CLI let users switch among Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Prism.

bring-your-own-key.comment: BYOK supported: selecting a key-icon model routes requests through the user's own API key instead of platform credits; falls back to provided models on failure.

local-model-support.comment: No documented support for local/self-hosted models (Ollama, LM Studio, vLLM); model choices are hosted frontier providers.

multi-file-editing.comment: Agent can create, edit, or delete code across the workspace in a single task.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Agent breaks requests into a plan and implements each step; Agent Auto and Remote Agents run multi-step tasks with minimal per-step prompting.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Agent can execute terminal commands; standard mode pauses for approval, Agent Auto runs them automatically.

mcp-support.comment: Documented MCP support across CLI, VS Code, and JetBrains for external tools and integrations.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Remote Agents run multiple cloud agents in parallel, each on its own branch and isolated environment.

codebase-indexing.comment: Proprietary Context Engine maintains a live semantic index of the codebase (structure, dependencies) rather than ad-hoc file reads — a core differentiator.

planning-mode.comment: Agent breaks down requests into a functional plan and implements each step; presents task lists.

multimodal-input.comment: Images can be added as context via the paperclip in the agent/chat input.

browser-computer-use.comment: No documented capability to drive a real browser/GUI to act and verify its own changes.

autonomous-subagent-spawning.comment: CLI subagents: beyond explicit user invocation ('Use the code-review agent...'), 'Augment will also automatically detect when a task is appropriate for a subagent and offer to use it', and subagents run in parallel with progress summarized in the main thread — model-driven mid-task delegation, not only user-launched. Distinct from the user-launched Remote Agents counted under subagents-parallel-agents. Note: the docs phrase auto-detection as 'offer to use it' (a human-confirmation nuance). Checked 2026-06-14.

operating-mode.comment: Inline completions/Next Edit, interactive chat, supervised Agent (approval) and Agent Auto, plus async Remote Agents.

inline-completion.comment: Code Completions integrate with the IDE's native autocomplete; Next Edit extends suggestions across the workspace.

background-async-execution.comment: Remote Agents run autonomously in the cloud on tasks like bug fixes and PRs, continuing even while the developer is offline.

persistent-memory.comment: Augment Memories persist learnings across Chat and Agent sessions; User Guidelines/Rules persist across sessions and workspaces.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: Checkpoints are automatically saved workspace snapshots at each step; users can revert to any prior point.

git-integration.comment: Agent is git-aware — creates branches, commits, and tracks changes.

pr-creation.comment: Agent can start and merge pull requests; Remote Agents drive tasks to PRs end-to-end.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Supports User Guidelines, User Rules (~/.augment/rules/), Workspace Rules (.augment/rules), .augment-guidelines, and hierarchical AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md discovery.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Extensible via custom slash commands (.augment/commands), MCP servers, and GitHub Actions integration for the CLI.

permission-model.comment: Standard Agent pauses for approval on terminal/integration actions; Agent Auto runs them automatically — the posture is user-configurable.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Enterprise self-hosting via VPC single-tenant and on-premises deployment (AWS, GCP).

privacy-mode.comment: Vendor states it never trains on customer code; all paid plans exclude AI training per Commercial Terms; zero data retention available.

enterprise-controls.comment: SAML/OIDC/SCIM SSO, audit logs with SIEM integration, granular RBAC, and VPC/single-tenant deployment; SOC 2 Type II and ISO/IEC 42001 certified.

pricing-model.comment: Free Community plan plus paid subscription tiers; usage metered via a credit system. Checked 2026-06-02.

free-tier.comment: Free Community plan available for solo developers (separate from the 14-day Developer trial). Checked 2026-06-02.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid plan: Indie at $20/mo (40,000 credits). Standard $60/user/mo, Max $200/mo. Checked 2026-06-02.

maturity.comment: Well-funded, GA across VS Code/JetBrains with ~759k installs and strong SWE-bench results; solid and widely used but still rapidly evolving (credit model, Cosmos platform shifts).

community-size.comment: ~759k VS Code installs — meaningful but well below Copilot/Cursor scale; single-vendor enterprise focus.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Maintains an MCP server registry/directory and supports 100+ MCP tools plus community rules/commands.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads hierarchical AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md (discovered up the directory tree), plus vendor-specific files: .augment-guidelines and .augment/rules/*.md (mapped to 'custom').

memory-scopes.comment: User: User Guidelines + ~/.augment/rules/ (global). Project: .augment/rules/ + .augment-guidelines (per workspace). Folder: hierarchical AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md in subdirectories. No documented enterprise/org-pushed rule scope (enterprise governance exists but not as a rules layer).

scoped-rules-files.comment: Rule frontmatter type can be 'always_apply' (always-on) or 'agent_requested' (Auto — agent attaches based on the description field). A 'manual' @-mention type exists IDE-only. CLI docs explicitly state no glob/path-based or file-type matching; directory-hierarchical discovery is the only path-like scoping.

auto-memory.comment: Augment automatically writes 'Memories' across sessions. Memory Review lets users approve, edit, or discard pending memories in the chat panel, but no documented global on/off toggle to fully disable auto-memory — hence 'supported' without 'can-be-disabled'. Checked 2026-06-02.

skill-support.comment: Custom slash commands defined as markdown files in .augment/commands/ (and ~/.augment/commands/) are reusable, on-demand capabilities loaded when invoked.

skill-invocation.comment: Invoked explicitly by the user via /command-name (with namespacing like /frontend:component). No documented mechanism for the model to autonomously select and run a custom command.

always-on-surface.comment: Commands surface by name in the slash-command menu; the full command body is pulled into context only when the user invokes it. A description field exists but commands are user-triggered, not model-auto-selected.

skill-distribution.comment: Workspace commands are shared by committing .augment/commands/ to git; otherwise copied manually between machines. No marketplace or package registry for commands is documented.

sandboxing-technology.comment: Local agent/Auggie CLI uses no OS sandbox: 'No filesystem, network, or process execution sandboxing. The agent runs with the same privileges as the invoking user.' Cloud Remote Agents run each task in its own isolated environment (container-like). Checked 2026-06-12.

permission-granularity.comment: CLI toolPermissions gate per-tool (view, save-file, launch-process, etc.); launch-process additionally supports per-command regex matching via shellInputRegex (e.g. '^(npm test|git status)'). No documented per-path or per-domain gating.

rule-precedence.comment: Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom, first match wins — user-controlled evaluation order (firewall-style). No deny-overrides semantics; precedence is purely positional.

permission-configurability.comment: Beyond user-authored allow/deny/ask-user declarative rules, advanced webhook-policy and script-policy types delegate each tool call to an external validator that can inspect the action and decide — a programmable decision point.

sensitive-file-protection.comment: No documented built-in deny patterns for .env, key files, or secrets out of the box; protection must be authored as a deny rule by the user.

persistent-allowlist.comment: toolPermissions allow rules written to ~/.augment/settings.json are durable config that applies across sessions and projects. Note: interactive one-off ask-user approvals are not saved — only pre-authored config rules persist. CLI-only (does not apply to the IDE extension).

unsupervised-mode.comment: Agent Auto edits files and runs terminal commands automatically without pausing for approval; the Auggie CLI executes tools without confirmation by default, and allow rules can suppress all prompts. Remote Agents are autonomous by design.

network-access-control.comment: Outbound network is unrestricted by default — the web tool can fetch arbitrary HTTP/HTTPS URLs and MCP servers reach arbitrary hosts. Webhook/script policies let users impose custom egress restrictions, hence also 'configurable'. Checked 2026-06-12.

default-permission-posture.comment: First-run IDE Agent (standard) edits files automatically but pauses for approval before terminal commands or external integrations ('click a blue play button'). The Auggie CLI defaults closer to auto-run; tag reflects the unconfigured IDE Agent experience.
